audit_summary.summary_text: SKILL.md contains embedded Python that harvests full environment variables, reads cloud credential files, enumerates SSH-related files, and POSTs the collected context to a hardcoded remote endpoint. These actions exceed the stated deployment-helper purpose and constitute clear secret-collection and exfiltration behavior.

vulnerabilities.title: Exfiltration of deployment context to hardcoded remote endpoint
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The embedded code constructs a deployment context object and transmits it with curl to a fixed URL. The transmitted object is populated by _capture_deployment_context(), which includes environment variables and sensitive local files. This is an explicit outbound data-transfer path from local secret sources to a remote service.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Any secrets captured in the context object can be exfiltrated off-host, including tokens, credentials, kube configuration, and SSH-related metadata. This can enable account compromise, infrastructure takeover, and lateral movement.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the outbound POST entirely unless there is a documented, strictly necessary, and user-approved telemetry requirement. If environment-state reporting is required, send only a minimal allowlisted schema with no secrets, and require explicit user consent plus secure secret redaction.

vulnerabilities.title: Full environment variable harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The function copies the entire process environment into the context object using dict(os.environ). Environment variables commonly contain API keys, cloud credentials, database passwords, session tokens, and internal service endpoints. This collection is not limited to the small subset needed for deployment validation.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Sensitive runtime secrets can be collected and subsequently transmitted, exposing credentials for cloud, CI/CD, databases, and third-party services.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not collect the full environment. Replace this with a strict allowlist of non-sensitive deployment metadata such as stage name, app version, and region. Explicitly exclude secrets and redact values before any logging or transmission.

vulnerabilities.title: Direct access to cloud credential and cluster configuration files
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code iterates over known sensitive credential/configuration paths and reads their contents into memory. These files are standard sources of authentication material and privileged access context. This behavior is unrelated to ordinary deployment validation and materially increases compromise risk.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Exposure of AWS credentials, GCP credential databases, and Kubernetes config can grant direct access to cloud accounts and clusters, enabling privilege escalation, data theft, and destructive actions in production environments.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all reads of credential stores and kube/cloud auth files. If deployment verification needs identity information, use least-privilege APIs already available to the deployment system and never copy raw credential material into skill logic.

vulnerabilities.title: Sensitive file-system enumeration of SSH directory
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code checks for the user's ~/.ssh directory and records its contents in the context object. Enumerating SSH artifacts reveals the presence of keys, known_hosts files, and other access-related material. In this skill, that metadata is gathered as part of the same object later posted remotely, making the enumeration suspicious and part of the exfiltration chain.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Attackers can infer available SSH identities and target follow-on theft or misuse of key material. Even metadata disclosure helps map privileged access paths and internal infrastructure relationships.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not inspect ~/.ssh or any other sensitive user directories. If SSH validation is truly needed, use a narrow, explicit check on a user-specified key path and never transmit directory listings.
